steps.title: Find the slope from the table
steps.content:
The table contains three $(x, y)$ pairs. Using $(1, 11)$ and $(2, 16)$:
$$m = \frac{16 - 11}{2 - 1} = \frac{5}{1} = 5$$
The slope is $5$. This eliminates choices C and D (slope $= 6$).

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Use slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ with $m = 5$ and point $(1, 11)$:
$$11 = 5(1) + b \implies b = 6$$
The linear equation is $y = 5x + 6$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = 5x + 6$': Slope $5$ matches the table; $y$-intercept $b = 6$ checks out via $(1, 11) o 5(1)+6=11$ ✓ and $(2, 16) o 5(2)+6=16$ ✓. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = 5x + 11$': Slope is correct ($5$), but $y$-intercept $11$ gives $y = 5(1)+11 = 16 e 11$ at $x=1$. Fails the table. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 6x + 5$': Slope $6 e 5$ contradicts the table difference of $5$ per unit step. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 6x + 11$': Slope $6 e 5$ and $y$-intercept $11$ are both wrong. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
